label: Brighthive 3.0
note: Umbrella release label, not a repo version. Each app repo keeps its own independent version field/tag scheme (see per-repo package.json/pyproject.toml + the vX.Y.Z.W-pre-release GitHub tag mechanism, both unchanged by this label). This file is the single source of truth mapping the '3.0' marketing/release number to the per-repo version each app was bumped to.

brighthive-admin.version_field: setuptools_scm (derived from git tags, no static field)
brighthive-admin.note: No git tags exist in this repo — setuptools_scm has nothing to derive a version from. Needs a v3.0.0 tag cut (deferred, see BH ticket for CI/CD + versioning follow-up).

deploy_mechanism_unaffected: vX.Y.Z.W-pre-release GitHub release tags on platform-core/webapp continue to drive deploy-staging.yml/deploy-production.yml exactly as before — this manifest sits on top as a label, never as a new tag format.

ci_cd_gate_audit_2026_07_21.summary: None of the 5 app repos has a real staging→main promote gate today (unit+integration+e2e+env-var-health). Tracked as follow-up under epic BH-170.

tickets.brightbot: BH-1140 — CI unit-test gate disabled (if: false)
tickets.brighthive-platform-core: BH-1141 — no integration/e2e gate, env-var Zod schema never validated in CI
tickets.brighthive-webapp: BH-1142 — deploy.yml 'test' job runs zero tests
tickets.brightbot-slack-server: BH-1143 — no test step before deploy (validate-deploy-config.mjs env-health check is the good pattern to copy)
tickets.brighthive-e2e: BH-1145 — health-check.yml has a real --gate mode but is wired to zero repos
